title: podman machine should automatically setup SSL based on SSL_CERT_FILE
body:
### Issue Description

This [guide](https://podman-desktop.io/docs/podman/adding-certificates-to-a-podman-machine) should not be necessary.

Podman should automatically detect `SSL_CERT_FILE` environment variable and setup certificates on startup.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use computer on network that does SSL interception like ZScaler
2. `export SSL_CERT_FILE=/path/to/ca.pem`
3. `podman machine start`
4. `podman run hello-world` 


### Describe the results you received

```
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...
Error: unable to copy from source docker://quay.io/podman/hello:latest: initializing source docker://quay.io/podman/hello:latest: pinging container registry quay.io: Get "https://quay.io/v2/": tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
```

### Describe the results you expected

Just works

### Privileged Or Rootless

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Also this is a quicker guide than in the docs:
```sh
podman machine ssh 'sudo -u root tee /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/ca.crt >/dev/null' < $SSL_CERT_FILE
podman machine ssh 'sudo -u root update-ca-trust'
podman machine stop
podman machine start
```
you should see this in podman 6
@l0rd care to close once you weigh in?

@mwisnicki, we are implementing a mechanism to automatically import certificates at machine startup (`podman machine init --import-native-ca`). It is described [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/design-docs/import-ca-certs.md) and [the first PR to implement it on Windows](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28336) is under review. Another PR, macOS-specific, will follow. I am closing this issue as that work should address it, but feel free to re-open.
@l0rd sounds good. May I suggest still implementing SSL_CERT_FILE as an override? There may be situations where importing entire system store is undesirable.

> here may be situations where importing entire system store is undesirable.

Importing the entire system store should not have drawbacks. Applications will trust the same certificates, no matter whether they run in the guest or the host. Why would that be undesirable?
